task: I’m getting my car ready before the next cold snap, and I want to make sure I don’t miss anything important. Can you help me put together a winter prep checklist I can work through before I head out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean winter prep checklist you can work through before heading out:

## Winter prep checklist

### 1) Trunk kit
- Ice scraper / snow brush
- Traction material: sand, kitty litter, or traction mats
- Flashlight with fresh batteries
- Phone charger / power bank
- Gloves
- Blanket or emergency warmth layer
- Jumper cables or jump pack
- Small shovel
- Water and a snack
- Reflective triangles or a way to mark the car if stuck
- First-aid kit
- Washer fluid rated for freezing weather

### 2) Before start-up
- Check tire pressure — cold temperatures lower it
- Make sure windshield washer fluid is topped off
- Confirm wipers are in good shape
- Check battery health if it’s old or weak
- Clear all snow and ice from windows, mirrors, lights, roof, and hood
- Make sure doors, locks, and fuel cap aren’t frozen shut

### 3) Before I pull out
- Test brakes lightly after moving a few feet
- Make sure headlights and taillights are on if visibility is poor
- Confirm mirrors and windows are fully clear
- Check that the route is safe and weather hasn’t worsened
- Leave extra following distance
- Drive slower than usual and avoid sudden braking or steering

If you want, I can also turn this into a tighter one-page version you can copy into your notes app or glove box.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. It’s clean enough to use, and it covers the stuff people usually skip. I’d still add a couple things for real cold weather — scraper, traction material, blanket, charger, flash light, and make sure the washer fluid is actually rated for the temps we’re getting, not just “winter” on the bottle. The roof and hood need to be fully cleared too, not half-assed.

If you want the tight version, make it usable in the truck — not a paragraph. Bullet list, no fluff.
